# AGENTS.md · 給 AI Coding Agent 的任務指引

This file follows the [AGENTS.md spec](https://agents.md/) — read this before doing any work in this repository.

## 0. AI 透明度（最重要的一條）

**This repository is openly AI-authored.** All non-question content was generated by Claude Code (Anthropic). When you (an AI agent) modify or extend this repo:

1. **Keep the AI disclosure visible.** Cover, colophon, footers, top-of-file blockquotes in each `handbook/*.md`, and the prominent block in `README.md` must continue to mention Claude Code authorship.
2. **Do NOT remove or weaken the "verify with Robbins / WHO" safety line** — it appears on every PDF page footer and in every handbook file. This is a medical-safety requirement.
3. **Do NOT add file-path citations to rendered output.** No `(source: foo.md)`, `[L42]`, `[出處: bar.json]` style references. The renderer has a regex strip + an assertion guard; do not bypass it.

These two rules are NOT in tension — AI signature is required; internal file path references are forbidden.

---

## 1. Repository purpose

Taiwanese pathology national exam (醫師國考病理科) cram-school study kit:
- 625 historical exam questions (104-1 through 115-1)
- 10 hand-curated study materials (cheatsheets, mock exams, flashcards)
- A Python render pipeline that produces beautiful cram-school-style PDFs

Target user: a pathology attending physician preparing residents/interns for the national board exam.

---

## 2. Directory map

```
data/                  Raw JSON question banks (immutable; do not edit content, only schema docs)
handbook/              10 study materials in Markdown + 1 CSV (00..09). These are the source of truth for PDFs.
rendered/              PDF output. Build artefact; regenerate with `python tools/render/render.py --all`.
tools/render/          Python pipeline. Edit here if rendering needs to change.
  ├─ config.py         Per-chapter settings table (orientation, fonts, accent colour)
  ├─ markdown_to_html.py  Includes citation-stripping regexes + path-leak audit
  ├─ csv_to_html.py    Grouped wide-table renderer for chapter 01
  ├─ html_template.py  Jinja2 wrappers (cover / colophon / divider / toc / body / back-cover)
  ├─ pdf_engine.py     Playwright wrapper that does the actual chromium-based PDF render
  └─ render.py         CLI entry point
assets/styles/         CSS (6 files, ordered 00..05)
assets/templates/      Jinja templates (6 files)
.claude/               Claude Code config + slash commands (only Claude Code reads this)
```

---

## 3. Build & verify

### One-time setup

```powershell
py -3.11 -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r tools/render/requirements.txt
python -m playwright install chromium
```

### Render

```powershell
python tools/render/render.py --all
python tools/render/render.py --file 03    # one chapter
python tools/render/render.py --master     # master edition only
python tools/render/render.py --clean      # wipe rendered/*.pdf
```

### Verify

A successful build produces 11 PDFs in `rendered/`:
- `00-overview.pdf` ... `09-weak-spot-cards.pdf` (10 individual chapters)
- `00-master-edition.pdf` (~128 pages, includes cover/colophon/TOC/back-cover)

Quick audit a PDF for path leaks:

```powershell
python -c "from pypdf import PdfReader; import re; r=PdfReader('rendered/00-master-edition.pdf'); t='\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in r.pages); print('leaks:', re.findall(r'(?:handbook|tools/render|assets/styles|data/)[\w./-]*|[\w\\-]+\\.(?:md|json|csv)', t)[:5] or 'NONE')"
```

Expected output: `leaks: NONE`.

---

## 4. When you change something

### Adding a new chapter
1. Add the source `.md` (or `.csv`) under `handbook/` with the next number prefix.
2. Append a chapter entry to `CHAPTERS` in `tools/render/config.py` (set `num`, `slug`, `source`, `title_zh`, `title_en`, `orient`, `kind`, `body_class`, `font_size_pt`).
3. Add an accent colour in `CHAPTER_ACCENT`.
4. Add the AI-disclosure blockquote at the top of the new handbook file (immediately under the `# Title`).
5. Re-run `python tools/render/render.py --all`.

### Updating an existing chapter
- Edit `handbook/<chapter>.md`. The AI-disclosure blockquote must remain immediately below the `# H1`.
- Re-render only the affected chapter with `--file NN`.

### Changing the visual style
- Edit `assets/styles/*.css`. The files are concatenated in order 00..05; later files override earlier ones.
- Per-chapter accent colours live in `tools/render/config.py:CHAPTER_ACCENT`.
- Cover and back-cover layout: `05_cover.css`.

### Changing the render engine
- `tools/render/pdf_engine.py` is the only file that touches Playwright. Keep it isolated.
- If you switch engines (e.g. back to WeasyPrint or to a new tool), preserve the `PageSpec` and `render_html_batch` API so the rest of the code doesn't change.

---

## 5. Hard rules

1. **AI signature stays.** Never delete the Claude Code attribution from cover, colophon, footers, or handbook blockquotes.
2. **No file-path citations in rendered output.** The `_assert_clean` guard in `render.py` will raise `RuntimeError` if any `.md`, `.json`, `.csv`, or `handbook/`-style path appears in the final HTML body. Do not weaken or bypass the audit.
3. **Preserve official exam codes.** Strings like `104-1`, `115-1-87`, `104-114` are legitimate exam-question identifiers; the audit regex is designed not to touch them.
4. **Do not touch `data/*.json`.** Those are immutable source data (official exam questions). Add new questions in new files; do not edit existing entries.
5. **No new heavyweight deps.** Stick to the 5 packages in `requirements.txt` (playwright, markdown-it-py, mdit-py-plugins, jinja2, pypdf). Adding TeX, pandoc, or wkhtmltopdf is out of scope.

---

## 6. Common pitfalls

- **`UnicodeEncodeError: 'cp950' codec`** on Windows: `tools/render/render.py` forces UTF-8 on stdout at startup. If you call render functions from another script, do the same.
- **Garbled CJK in `pypdf.extract_text()`** on chapter 01: this is a known pypdf limitation with Chromium's subset font embedding. The PDF itself displays correctly. Don't try to "fix" it by changing fonts.
- **Chromium's `@page` ignores `@top-left`/`@bottom-right`**: page headers/footers come from Playwright's `headerTemplate`/`footerTemplate` (in `html_template.py:header_template`/`footer_template`), NOT from CSS.

---
